_doc: The shared enforcement registry — ONE authored copy of every guard, read by every platform's thin shim. A rule carries INTENT + CLASS + which neutral act it watches + the PREDICATE that decides + any DATA params. The predicate names a function in the shared engine (src/adapter/engine.mjs); the engine holds the logic, this file holds the rules and their data. A platform adapter normalises its harness input to {act, path, command, content, spawnTarget, actor} and asks the engine to evaluate; the engine returns allow | deny | ask | inject. A platform that cannot realise a class (e.g. OpenCode has no 'ask' return) FALLS BACK to persona for that rule — recorded in `fallback`, never silently dropped. Adding a platform = write its input-normaliser + map the three return classes; touch nothing here.

classes.ask: block and ask the Operator to confirm; a platform with no ask-return falls back to persona
classes.inject: do not block; add a context note to the turn

role_deny_set._doc: Single home for the role-duplicator + generic-builtin deny set. A spawn whose target is in this list never fills a protocol seat. code-review / deep-research and wb-* KNOWLEDGE skills are NOT here — they are designated engines/aids, not seat substitutes.

change_verbs._doc: Used by the change-route-reminder (inject). RU+EN verbs that signal a repo-change request.

orchestrator_writable._doc: Paths the orchestrator session MAY author (everything else source/canonical is sub-agent-only). Covers its state, backlog, the transient plan files (.ai-pm/plans/), and the project config. The tooling submodule is never writable (self-patch guard).

rules.intent: Block a read whose target resolves outside the project root.
Block a `find` that searches outside the project root.
Block any write whose target resolves outside the project root (write/edit and bash redirect, tee, sed -i, cp/mv dest, dd of=).
Block an empty/whitespace-only write over an existing non-empty file (truncation guard; 2026-06-06 doc-loss regression).

Block the orchestrator session writing a source/canonical-doc path; sub-agents author content, the orchestrator routes. Allowed: its own state and a feature plan, and any source/doc when the project's `profile` is lite/solo (configurable rigor — the orchestrator builds directly; the floor denies below are never relaxed). Fails OPEN on an unknown actor (never a false denial).
Block any write into the tooling submodule — the enforcer's own source + the deployed adapter change only via a git submodule bump, never an in-place edit.

Block a git merge/push of any branch (prefix stripped — feature/, fix/, hotfix/, …) whose review file lacks a satisfied review/validation stamp. Checks stamp PRESENCE, not authorship (fabrication stays persona). Fails OPEN on ambiguity.
Block spawning a role-duplicator or a generic built-in into a protocol seat (role_deny_set). The designated pm-* role / code-review / deep-research are never blocked.
Block an in-place content edit on a remote system (ssh + sed -i / vi / vim / nano / tee / > redirect). Repo-owned files change via git, never a remote in-place edit.
rules.fallback: persona on a platform without remote-edit detection

rules.intent: Confirm before a mutating action on a remote system (ssh + systemctl/docker/apt/npm/kubectl/rm/cp/mv/mkdir/touch). Legitimate for deploy/maintenance/runtime-state; never for editing a repo-owned file.
rules.fallback: persona on a platform with no ask-return (e.g. OpenCode)

rules.intent: Confirm a git push --force / -f / --force-with-lease — it rewrites remote history; never force-push main.
rules.fallback: persona on a platform with no ask-return

rules.intent: Confirm a git commit --no-verify / --no-gpg-sign — it skips pre-commit hooks or signing; fix the failing hook instead of bypassing it.
rules.fallback: persona on a platform with no ask-return

rules.intent: This project is not configured (no ai-pm.config.json). Before doing the task, offer the Operator exactly two choices — run `/pm-setup` now, or proceed on the safe defaults — then STOP and wait for their answer. Do not start the task, do not explore the repo, do not run git. Keep the offer short.
rules.fallback: always-on instruction text on a platform with no prompt hook

rules.intent: On a repo-change request (change_verbs), inject a reminder to follow the loop (plan -> build -> review -> ship) and route work to the owning role, not the orchestrator. Fires once the project is configured (an unconfigured one gets no-config-run-setup first).
rules.fallback: always-on instruction text on a platform with no prompt hook
